touches.ai_summary: Outbound LinkedIn message sent to Dylan Lopez introducing AskElephant's onboarding automation capabilities. No response received.
Follow-up LinkedIn message to Dylan Lopez sharing a case study on RevOps automation. No response received.
Third LinkedIn touchpoint mentioning ClearAxis's recent growth and onboarding challenges in managed IT. No response received.

Dylan responded and scheduled qualification call. Discussed current onboarding process pain points at ClearAxis. Dylan managing 229 employees' onboarding manually, taking 8-12 hours per new hire. Interested in automation to scale RevOps. Currently evaluating Fireflies as alternative. Agreed to deeper discovery call.

questions_asked.question: What's your current onboarding process like for new hires?
questions_asked.answer: Completely manual—I'm coordinating across IT, HR, and department heads. Takes me 8-12 hours per employee to get everything provisioned and documented.

questions_asked.question: What prompted you to look at solutions now?
questions_asked.answer: We grew from 180 to 229 employees in 6 months. The manual process doesn't scale, and I'm spending half my week on onboarding instead of strategic RevOps work.

questions_asked.question: How does AskElephant compare to Fireflies for onboarding use cases?
questions_asked.answer: Fireflies is primarily meeting transcription. AskElephant is purpose-built for workflow automation with deeper integrations into HRIS, ticketing, and provisioning systems.

next_steps: Dylan to share current onboarding workflow documentation
Schedule 30-min discovery call with implementation team

touches.ai_summary: Attempted cold call to Dylan Lopez. No answer, left voicemail referencing qualification conversation.
LinkedIn message thanking Dylan for qualification call. Dylan responded confirming interest and availability for discovery next week.
Brief follow-up call to confirm discovery meeting logistics. Dylan introduced Finn Ahmed and Amelia Lewis from implementation team who will join. Discussed agenda for deeper technical discovery.

questions_asked.question: Will you be covering integrations with our existing stack—BambooHR and ServiceNow?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we'll walk through native integrations and can do a live demo of the provisioning workflow.

next_steps: Send calendar invite including Finn and Amelia
Dylan to provide tech stack documentation

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent to Dylan with discovery meeting agenda and pre-work questionnaire about current tech stack, integration points, and success metrics. Dylan responded same day with completed questionnaire.

questions_asked.question: Can you share ROI case studies specific to managed IT companies?
questions_asked.answer: Attached two case studies from similar mid-market MSPs showing 60-70% time savings on onboarding workflows.

next_steps: Review Dylan's tech stack documentation before discovery

touches.ai_summary: Email from Dylan introducing Finn Ahmed and Amelia Lewis. Both confirmed attendance for discovery call. Amelia asked about implementation timeline.

questions_asked.question: What's typical implementation timeline for a company our size?
questions_asked.answer: For 11 seats with standard integrations, typically 3-4 weeks from contract signature to go-live.

touches.ai_summary: Discovery and demo call with Dylan, Finn, and Amelia. Walked through current onboarding workflow pain points—manual ticket creation, lack of visibility, inconsistent provisioning timelines. Demonstrated AskElephant's workflow automation, BambooHR integration, and ServiceNow ticketing. Team engaged, asked detailed technical questions. Finn noted procurement process requires additional stakeholder review. No executive sponsor identified yet. Dylan mentioned Fireflies quote is significantly lower but lacks automation depth.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in the decision-making process?
questions_asked.answer: Finn and Amelia handle procurement and implementation approval. We may need buy-in from our CTO for broader automation initiatives, but Dylan has budget authority for RevOps tools.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the onboarding workflows by department—IT vs sales vs customer success?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully customizable workflows with conditional logic based on role, department, location, or any HRIS field.

questions_asked.question: How does pricing compare to Fireflies for our use case?
questions_asked.answer: Fireflies is lower cost but focused on transcription. AskElephant pricing reflects the automation platform capabilities—we're typically 30-40% higher but deliver 10x the workflow value.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for you in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Onboarding time cut in half, full visibility into provisioning status, and freeing up 15-20 hours per week for strategic work.

objections: Pricing appears higher than Fireflies—need to justify ROI to procurement
next_steps: Send proposal with pricing for 11 seats
Include ROI calculator and Fireflies comparison
Dylan to schedule internal review with Finn and Amelia

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Dylan. Walked through $11,680 ACV pricing for 11 seats (automations_plus_consulting tier). Dylan raised concerns about budget—target was closer to $8,000 based on Fireflies pricing. Discussed value differentiation and implementation support. Dylan appreciates the capabilities but needs to work within budget constraints. Agreed to explore pricing flexibility.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing to get closer to our budget target?
questions_asked.answer: We have some room to work with, especially if you can commit to annual contract and provide a case study. Let me discuss with leadership.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the $8K budget target—is that based on the Fireflies quote?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, Fireflies came in around $7,500 annually. I know it's not apples-to-apples, but that's the benchmark procurement is using.

objections: Pricing 50% above budget target and Fireflies alternative
next_steps: Rep to explore discount options with leadership
Dylan to confirm annual vs monthly preference
Schedule pricing follow-up call

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled pricing negotiation call was rescheduled by Dylan due to internal budget review meeting conflict. Brief conversation confirmed continued interest but need to finalize budget allocation. Dylan mentioned Grace Santos from implementation team will join next pricing discussion as end-user representative.
next_steps: Reschedule pricing call for next week
Include Grace Santos in next meeting

touches.ai_summary: Exec alignment meeting with Dylan. Discussed strategic value of onboarding automation for ClearAxis's growth trajectory. Dylan confirmed no CTO involvement needed—this sits within his RevOps budget authority. Walked through ROI projections showing $42K annual savings vs $8,900 investment. Dylan comfortable with value but still needs to reconcile pricing with procurement's Fireflies comparison. Agreed to 23.8% discount to bring ACV to $8,900, contingent on annual contract and case study participation.

questions_asked.question: Is there an executive sponsor we should loop in to ensure alignment on strategic automation initiatives?
questions_asked.answer: I have full authority on RevOps tooling decisions. Our CTO is focused on product and infrastructure—this is my domain.

questions_asked.question: Can you formalize the $8,900 pricing in a revised proposal?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, I'll send updated proposal today reflecting annual contract at $8,900 ACV with implementation support and quarterly business reviews.

next_steps: Send revised proposal at $8,900 ACV
Dylan to review with Finn, Amelia, and Grace
Schedule final pricing call to address any remaining questions

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing and terms call with Dylan, Finn, Amelia, and Grace. Reviewed revised proposal at $8,900 ACV for 11 seats with annual contract. Team walked through implementation plan, success metrics, and case study participation requirements. Grace confirmed onboarding workflow requirements align with demo. Finn and Amelia approved procurement terms. Dylan committed to moving forward pending legal review of contract terms—expects 1-week turnaround. Discussed implementation kickoff timeline for early May.

questions_asked.question: What's included in the implementation support at this pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Full implementation support includes workflow configuration, integration setup, 2 training sessions, and dedicated CSM for first 90 days.

questions_asked.question: Are there any additional fees we should anticipate—overages, premium integrations, etc.?
questions_asked.answer: No hidden fees. 11 seats covers your current team, standard integrations included, and you can add seats at $809 per seat annually if you grow.

questions_asked.question: What does legal review typically focus on—any terms that have been sticking points in past vendor contracts?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly data security and SLA terms. We use standard MSA templates, so shouldn't be major issues. I'll flag if anything comes up.

objections: Need to confirm data residency requirements with IT security team
next_steps: Dylan to route contract to legal for review
Rep to provide data security documentation for IT review
Follow up in 3-4 days on legal review status
Target contract signature by end of next week
